scenario: Happy path — sprint is ready, working tree is clean, local main matches origin/main, the sprint branch is created and pushed on the first attempt, and the user confirms delegation to /sprint-deliver.
preconditions: Target project's CLAUDE.md contains the Nexa Rules Gate marker
docs/sprints/next-sprint/ exists with a readiness-report.md present
No existing docs/sprints/sprint-N/ folders (so the new sprint number is sprint-1)
git status --porcelain returns empty (clean working tree)
git fetch origin main followed by git diff main..origin/main --stat shows no differences
expected_steps: Step 1: docs/sprints/next-sprint/ and its readiness-report.md both found
Step 2: no existing docs/sprints/sprint-*/ folders found, so sprint number N = 1
Step 3: sprint summary displayed (scope, delivery order, pre-delivery action status, blockers/warnings)

Step 4: git status --porcelain confirmed clean; git fetch origin main + diff show local main up to date; git checkout -b sprint-1 run; git push -u origin sprint-1 run; branch creation confirmed to the user
Step 5: user asked 'Ready to kick off Sprint 1?' with branch/scope/delivery order shown; user confirms
expected_terminal_outcome: sprint-1 branch exists locally and on origin, is checked out, and /sprint-deliver has been invoked to begin autonomous delivery.
pass_criteria: The branch is created from an up-to-date main with no uncommitted changes carried over; the user is asked to confirm exactly once before delegation; /sprint-deliver is only invoked after that confirmation, never before or unconditionally.
